caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied 4-digit occupational mix spans service models within 621610.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Fractions measure mature AI task assistance/replacement, not legacy non-AI automation or avoided visits.
inputs.pi_dist: Applies to a skilled Medicare-oriented target, not Medicaid HCBS rates or all MA contracts.
inputs.pi_moat: No agency-specific referral concentration or payer-contract evidence is available.
inputs.t50_years: This estimates adoption of useful administrative/clinical-support AI, not autonomous clinical care.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: No current representative six-digit survey measures effective AI deployment at $1–10M EBITDA agencies.
inputs.historical_analogs: The sources do not establish a historical causal adoption curve for generative AI.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Use as a weak buyability assumption; validate owner age locally before underwriting.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: This is a market-structure judgment, not a documented nationwide owner-survey result.
inputs.active_consolidators: Not a transaction-database count and varies by skilled, private-duty and hospice service line.
inputs.buy_mult: Advisory benchmark; no disclosed $1–10M EBITDA transaction comp is supplied.
inputs.exit_mult: Exit assumes no adverse reimbursement or MA-network deterioration and is not a promised multiple.
inputs.pricing_structure: No payer mix for the target EBITDA band is available.
The cited scheduling-technology survey does not establish AI/ML revenue-cycle use or effective AI adoption, and the BLS crosswalk confirms the code/title rather than the memo's detailed service mix.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1580 (margins.json[62]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Hospitals/Healthcare Facilities EBITDA/Sales 15.80% (closest named bucket for provider economics; public support-services bucket at 3.87% reflects a different business model)); revenue/employee $72,294 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 621610); class <5: 11,187 firms, avg revenue/firm $242,470 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $38,310 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 3,386 firms, avg revenue/firm $660,235 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $104,317 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 3,828 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,118,957 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $176,795 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 7,049 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,771,668 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $437,923 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 1,983 firms, avg revenue/firm $10,545,427 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,666,177 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 765 firms, avg revenue/firm $83,228,652 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $13,150,127 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 1,983

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: Only administrative, documentation, scheduling and revenue-cycle tasks are meaningfully AI-addressable. Hands-on care and clinical visits dominate the supplied mix; estimates preserve that distinction.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 621610 ($71,424,773,339) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 621610 ($113,350,417,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics, digests, dataset injection, fresh finalization, memo rendering and arithmetic all pass. The supported direction is durable demand with modest AI-addressable labor and constrained capture; citation overreach and disclosed proxy/market uncertainties are non-fatal, so the record is publishable with caveats.

sources.title: QCEW NAICS Hierarchy Crosswalk
sources.what_it_supports: Confirms 621610 is Home Health Care Services within ambulatory health care; scope framing is supplemented by the frozen prompt and disclosed as heterogeneous.

sources.title: Calendar Year 2026 Home Health Prospective Payment System Final Rule
sources.what_it_supports: CY 2026 PDGM permanent -1.023% and temporary -3.0% payment adjustments and the rate-rebasing channel.

sources.title: MedPAC March 2026 Report to the Congress, Chapter 14
sources.what_it_supports: 2023 FFS home-health margin evidence, covered-service context, workforce constraints and Medicare Advantage mix risk.

sources.title: Alliance for Care at Home RFI on AI in Health Care
sources.what_it_supports: Home-based-care AI opportunities and material implementation, integration, privacy, workforce and legal barriers.

sources.title: 2025 HHAeXchange Survey on Caregiver Technology and Retention
sources.what_it_supports: Caregiver-reported technology use in scheduling and communication; it is a homecare survey, not a direct measure of AI use in all 621610 agencies.

sources.title: Q4 2025 Home-Based Care M&A Report
sources.what_it_supports: 2025 home-based-care transaction volume, active sponsor-backed buyers and service-line-specific M&A activity.

sources.title: Home Care Business EBITDA Multiples: 2026 Benchmark Report
sources.what_it_supports: Published benchmark range of 4.5x–8x EBITDA for Medicare-certified skilled home health; advisory-source benchmark, not a deal-specific comp set.

title: Home Health Care Services
